amendment_2: Will's ruling (b) 2026-06-12 (brief 7A Amendment 2): the dry-run gains EXACT domain membership (Q denominator != 0 at every stencil node); excision is RULE-BASED via tower.stencil_in_domain (single definition, generator and runner both consume) - never hand-enumerated. STANDING LAW: a dual matching typed refusal at an exactly-verified domain violation is a lawful domain event, not an H1 counterexample; any POST-v2 runtime refusal is an anomaly -> fork 1.7, route to Will, do not proceed.
annex_exclusion: C1/C2/T1/T2/T3 (true_class annex) are OUTSIDE Stage A entirely (Amendment 1 DP1).
authority: Build_Docs/Agent_tasks/CAMPAIGN_DIFFERENCE_TOWER.md sections 7 (H1, H3), 7A Amendment 1, 9 Stage A; Will's Stage-A GO 2026-06-12 (prereg-first; kills K1/K2 live)
certificate_semantics: terminates(F, m) := every (point, scheme, j) cell of F's grid has Delta^m == 0 in Q. certificate degree d*(F) := min{m-1 : terminates(F, m)} over F's m-grid, if any. FALSE POSITIVE (= K2 kill): a certificate fires on any h3_negative fixture, OR fires with d* < declared d on an h3_positive fixture.
determinism: byte-stable re-run x2 (sha256 over records) required before the report; records sha-pinned in the verdicts file; zero RNG anywhere.
dp2_logging: Amendment 1 DP2 standing instruction: every BOUNDED / below_sufficient_condition firing location is logged in stage records (expected count on this grid: 0 cells); any such firing on a rational-class fixture goes to the surprise ledger IMMEDIATELY.
grid_notes: points = frozen Stage-0 gate points per fixture + extra_points; j = tight ladder for the RN(1/10) point, j_override if set, else wide; ALL cells validated at freeze time for BOTH lattice realizability AND exact domain membership (Amendment 2), with out-of-domain cells excised by rule, so zero refusals are now a SOUND expectation: any runtime refusal is an anomaly (fork discipline section 1.7, route to Will).
h1_rule: for every rational-class fixture, every grid cell (point, scheme, j, m): lane_tower_cell == referee_tower_cell, bit-for-bit in Q. Zero violations. ANY inequality = K1: HALT campaign for Will. Units: cells = (fixture, point, scheme, j, m) tuples.
h3_negative_rule: for each negative fixture and each pinned probe order m*: Delta^(m*) != 0 in Q at SOME cell of its (point, scheme, j) grid. Exact grading.
h3_positive_rule: for each positive fixture of declared degree d: (a) Delta^(d+1) == 0 in Q at EVERY (point, scheme, j) cell of its grid; (b) for d >= 1: Delta^d != 0 at SOME cell (degree exactness - the certificate must not under-shoot). Exact grading, zero band tests.
v2_scope: the H1 battery only; H3 v1 verdicts STAND by Will's ruling (cells unaffected, rules unbroken) and are carried forward by citation, not re-run.
